audit_items.proof_trace: Neither `add_ref` nor `free` in tran_buf_pool.h contain internal NULL guards, but all callers enforce the precondition. `TranBufPool::malloc()` results are null-checked at every call site before use (Extend() at buffer.cc:284, reset_tran_buf() at rpc_message_stream.h:696), and `free` is always guarded by `if (_tran_buf != NULL)` in destructors and reset_tran_buf(). The network-input path populates `_tran_buf` only after a successful allocation and closes the connection on failure, so `split_and_process_message` always operates on a non-NULL `_tran_buf`.
